narrative_summary: The BRCA2 missense change p.Lys1434Ile (c.4301A>T) is a variant of uncertain significance that has been investigated in several studies. Functional testing in a mouse embryonic stem‑cell complementation assay demonstrated that the variant restores cell viability and shows near‑normal homologous‑directed repair activity, findings that are interpreted as evidence for a benign effect [PMC6752316]. A re‑evaluation of BRCA2 missense variants placed p.K1434I in IARC Class 2, indicating it is likely not pathogenic, and confirmed earlier neutral categorisation by Spearman et al. [PMC3478957]. The same amino‑acid change was also reported among a list of BRCA2 alterations identified in a familial pancreatic‑cancer cohort, but the report did not assign any pathogenicity interpretation to it [PMC4439391]. No population‑frequency data or computational predictions were provided in the extracted evidence. Taken together, the available clinical classification and functional assay results consistently support a benign or likely benign classification for BRCA2 K1434I, and there is no contradictory evidence suggesting pathogenicity.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study demonstrates that the mouse embryonic stem cell functional assay can assess BRCA2 VUS, with K1434I showing complementation and near‑normal HDR activity, suggesting it is likely benign.
article_mentions.title: The functional impact of variants of uncertain significance in BRCA2

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study found that deleterious BRCA2 mutations occur in 3.7% of familial pancreatic cancer probands, indicating BRCA2’s substantial contribution to disease risk.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, and CDKN2A Mutations in Familial Pancreatic Cancer (FPC): A PACGENE Study

mentions.quoted_text: A group of four BRCA2 missense substitutions assessed by Spearman et al. (Spearman et al., 2008) are now classified as Class 2. Two of these, **p.K1434I** and p.A2351G were originally placed in Spearman et al’s “neutral suspected category”; for these, our analysis simply confirms Spearman et al’s. Of the remaining two, p.D1352Y moved into Class 2 because our model excluded LOH data that had provided some evidence in favor of pathogenicity, and p.R2418G moved into Class 2 because our “likely not pathogenic” category extends up to a posterior probability of 0.05 whereas the suspect neutral category used by Spearman et al was more restrictive.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article re‑evaluates BRCA2 missense variants and confirms that p.K1434I belongs to IARC Class 2, indicating it is likely benign.
article_mentions.title: Classification of Missense Substitutions in the BRCA Genes: a Database Dedicated to Ex-UVs
